8	/-!
9	# Stalk of a tensor product of presheaves of modules (ingredient d.2)
10	
11	This file constructs the *comparison map* underlying the varying-ring stalk--tensor
12	commutation isomorphism
13	
14	  `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x ≅ A.stalk x ⊗_{R.stalk x} B.stalk x`
15	
16	for presheaves of modules over the structure presheaf `R` of a scheme — the single
17	genuinely Mathlib-absent ingredient ("d.2") on which the unconditional associator of
18	the relative-Picard tensor substrate rests.
19	
20	This iteration builds the forward additive comparison map `stalkTensorDesc` and its
21	germ characterisation (the colimit-descent of the sectionwise base-change maps). The
22	full isomorphism `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorIso` (blueprint `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`)
23	additionally requires the `R.stalk x`-linear packaging of this map, the reverse map, and
24	their mutual inversion on germs — see the handoff in `task_results`.
25	
26	The construction proceeds bottom-up:
27	
28	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorBilin` — for a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the `R(U)`-balanced
29	  bilinear map `A(U) × B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, `(a,b) ↦ germ a ⊗ germ b`.
30	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDescU` — its descent through `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`
31	  to a map `(A ⊗ᵖ B)(U) = A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`.
32	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDesc` — the colimit descent to the comparison map
33	  `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`.
34	
35	Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma `lemma-stalk-tensor-product`.
36	
37	Blueprint: `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, §`sec:tensorobj_stalk_tensor`
38	(`lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`).
39	-/
40	
41	set_option autoImplicit false
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
46	
47	namespace PresheafOfModules
48	
49	open PresheafOfModules.Monoidal
50	
51	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — stalk of a tensor product (d.2) -/
52	
53	section StalkTensor
54	
55	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
56	  (A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (x : X)
57	
58	local notation3 "Tgt" =>
59	  TensorProduct ↑(R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk A.presheaf x)
60	    ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x)
61	
62	/-- **The `R(U)`-balanced bilinear comparison map.** For a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the
63	bilinear (additive-monoid-hom) map `A(U) × B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` sending
64	`(a, b)` to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. Project-local: the building block of the d.2 stalk--tensor
65	comparison; Mathlib has no varying-ring stalk--tensor commutation. -/
66	noncomputable def stalkTensorBilin (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
67	    (↑(A.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+ (↑(B.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+ Tgt where
68	  toFun m :=
69	    ((TensorProduct.mk ↑(R.stalk x) _ _
70	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) m)).toAddMonoidHom).comp
71	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx))
72	  map_zero' := by ext n; simp
73	  map_add' m m' := by ext n; simp
74	
75	/-- The defining balancing identity making `stalkTensorBilin` descend through the
76	relative tensor product `A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U)`: `(r • a) ⊗ b ↦ a ⊗ (r • b)`. -/
77	lemma stalkTensorBilin_balanced (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (r : ↑(R.obj (op U)))
78	    (m : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (n : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
79	    (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx (r • m)) n = (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx m) (r • n) := by
80	  simp only [stalkTensorBilin, AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp,
81	    LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, Function.comp_apply, TensorProduct.mk_apply]
82	  rw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x U hx]
83	  erw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul B x U hx]
84	  exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _
85	
86	/-- **The per-neighbourhood descended comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B)(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`,
87	obtained from `stalkTensorBilin` via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. The source object is
88	`(A ⊗ᵖ B).presheaf.obj (op U)`, definitionally `A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U)`. -/
89	noncomputable def stalkTensorDescU (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
90	    (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt :=
91	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
92	    (TensorProduct.liftAddHom (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx) (stalkTensorBilin_balanced A B x U hx))
93	
94	@[simp] lemma stalkTensorDescU_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
95	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
96	    (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b)
97	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
98	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b := by
99	  change (TensorProduct.liftAddHom (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx)
100	    (stalkTensorBilin_balanced A B x U hx)) (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b) = _
101	  rw [TensorProduct.liftAddHom_tmul]
102	  rfl
103	
104	/-- **Scalar compatibility of the per-neighbourhood descent.** For `r : R(U)` and a
105	section `z` of `A ⊗ᵖ B` over `U`, the descent `stalkTensorDescU` carries the `R(U)`-scalar
106	action over to the `R.stalk x`-action by the germ of `r`. This is stage (iii)'s
107	section-level core; the carrier-duality between the `CommRingCat` scalar `r : R(U)` and the
108	`RingCat`-carrier module structure on `A(U) ⊗ B(U)` is absorbed by `germ_smul`/`smul_tmul'`. -/
109	lemma stalkTensorDescU_smul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (r : ↑(R.obj (op U)))
110	    (z : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).obj (op U))) :
111	    (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) (r • z)
112	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ R U x hx)) r •
113	          (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) z := by
114	  -- The section tensor `A(U) ⊗ B(U)` is a module over the `RingCat` carrier
115	  -- `S := (R ⋙ forget₂).obj (op U)`, which is defeq to the `CommRingCat` carrier `R(U)`.
116	  -- We pin `smul_tmul'`/`smul_add`/`smul_zero` to that ring so the scalar `r` slots in.
117	  induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
118	  | zero =>
119	      simp only [presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj,
120	        Monoidal.tensorObj_obj, map_zero, smul_zero]
121	      exact map_zero _
122	  | tmul a b =>
123	      erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x U hx]
124	      exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm
125	  | add p q hp hq =>
126	      erw [smul_add]
127	      refine (map_add _ _ _).trans ?_
128	      rw [hp, hq, ← smul_add]
129	      exact congrArg _ (map_add _ p q).symm
130	
131	/-- **The d.2 comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, obtained by descending
132	the per-neighbourhood maps `stalkTensorDescU` through the colimit defining the stalk. -/
133	noncomputable def stalkTensorDesc :
134	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt :=
135	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf)
136	    ⟨AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt,
137	      fun U => stalkTensorDescU A B x (unop U).1 (unop U).2,
138	      by
139	        intro U V i
140	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
141	        ext z
142	        induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
143	        | zero => exact (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).symm
144	        | tmul a b =>
145	            simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
146	            erw [tensorObj_map_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul]
147	            erw [germ_res_apply, germ_res_apply]
148	            rfl
149	        | add p q hp hq =>
150	            exact (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).trans
151	              ((congrArg₂ HAdd.hAdd hp hq).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).symm)⟩
152	
153	/-- The comparison map `stalkTensorDesc` factors the per-neighbourhood map
154	`stalkTensorDescU` through the germ, by the colimit universal property. -/
155	@[reassoc]
156	lemma germ_stalkTensorDesc (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
157	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx ≫ stalkTensorDesc A B x
158	      = stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx :=
159	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨U, hx⟩ : OpenNhds x))
160	
161	/-- **Germ characterisation of the d.2 comparison map** on a simple tensor: the germ of
162	`a ⊗ b` over `U` maps to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. -/
163	@[simp]
164	lemma stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
165	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
166	    (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
167	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx))
168	          (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b))
169	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
170	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b := by
171	  rw [← stalkTensorDescU_tmul, ← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]
172	
173	/-- **Element form of `germ_stalkTensorDesc`.** The comparison map sends the germ of a
174	section `w` over `U` to its per-neighbourhood descent `stalkTensorDescU`. -/
175	lemma stalkTensorDesc_germ (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
176	    (w : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U))) :
177	    (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
178	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx)) w)
179	      = (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) w := by
180	  rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]
181	
182	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear d.2 comparison map** (stage (iii)). The additive comparison
183	map `stalkTensorDesc` is `R.stalk x`-linear for the stalk module structures of
184	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`: linearity is checked on a germ `germ r` of a
185	scalar `r : R(U)` via the germ--scalar compatibility `germ_smul` and the section-level
186	scalar compatibility `stalkTensorDescU_smul`, after passing to a common neighbourhood.
187	This is the linear packaging the d.2 stalk--tensor isomorphism `stalkTensorIso` builds on. -/
188	noncomputable def stalkTensorLinearMap :
189	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x) : Type u)
190	      →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)] Tgt where
191	  toFun := ConcreteCategory.hom (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
192	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
193	  map_smul' r ξ := by
194	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
195	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
196	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, z, rfl⟩ :=
197	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x ξ
198	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
199	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
200	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
201	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
202	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
203	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
204	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
205	    have hz : (ConcreteCategory.hom
206	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf V x hxV)) z
207	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom
208	            (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf W x hxW))
209	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.map iWV.op)) z) :=
210	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf iWV x hxW z).symm
211	    rw [hr, hz, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul (Monoidal.tensorObj A B) x W hxW,
212	      stalkTensorDesc_germ, stalkTensorDescU_smul, ← stalkTensorDesc_germ]
213	
214	/-- **Germ characterisation of the linear comparison map** on a simple tensor: the germ of
215	`a ⊗ b` over `U` maps to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. The `R.stalk x`-linear repackaging
216	`stalkTensorLinearMap` agrees with `stalkTensorDesc` on germs. -/
217	@[simp]
218	lemma stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
219	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
220	    stalkTensorLinearMap A B x
221	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx))
222	          (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b))
223	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
224	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b :=
225	  stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul A B x U hx a b
226	
227	/-! ### Stage (iv): the reverse map -/
228	
229	/-- **Inner cocone leg for the reverse map** (fixed `A`-section `a` over `U`). For a
230	`B`-neighbourhood `V ∋ x`, the additive map `B(V) → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` sending `b` to the
231	germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. Project-local building block of the d.2 reverse map. -/
232	private noncomputable def revInnerLeg (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
233	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) :
234	    B.presheaf.obj (op V) ⟶ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x :=
235	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom <|
236	    (ConcreteCategory.hom
237	        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩)).comp
238	      (((TensorProduct.mk ↑(R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))) ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V))) ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))
239	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
240	            ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))).toAddMonoidHom).comp
241	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)))
242	
243	/-- Evaluation of `revInnerLeg`: it sends `b` to the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
244	private lemma revInnerLeg_apply (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
245	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.presheaf.obj (op V))) :
246	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a V hxV)) b
247	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
248	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
249	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
250	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
251	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
252	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) :=
253	  rfl
254	
255	/-- **Inner descent of the reverse map** (fixed `A`-section `a` over `U`): the additive
256	map `B.stalk x → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` descending `revInnerLeg` through the `B`-stalk colimit. -/
257	private noncomputable def revInner (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) :
258	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x ⟶
259	      TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x :=
260	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ B.presheaf)
261	    ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x,
262	      fun W => revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a (unop W).1 (unop W).2,
263	      by
264	        intro W W' f
265	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
266	        ext b
267	        simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
268	        erw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.id_apply, revInnerLeg_apply]
269	        conv_rhs => erw [revInnerLeg_apply]
270	        have hVle : (unop W').1 ≤ (unop W).1 := leOfHom ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).map f.unop)
271	        have j : U ⊓ (unop W').1 ⟶ U ⊓ (unop W).1 := homOfLE (inf_le_inf_left U hVle)
272	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf j x
273	          ⟨hxU, (unop W').2⟩]
274	        congr 1
275	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
276	        congr 1
277	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, ← presheaf_map_apply_coe]
278	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
279	          congr 1
280	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, Functor.comp_map]
281	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
282	            ← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
283	          congr 1⟩
284	
285	/-- The inner descent factors `revInnerLeg` through the `B`-germ, by the colimit
286	universal property. -/
287	private lemma germ_revInner (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
288	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) :
289	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV ≫ revInner A B x U hxU a
290	      = revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a V hxV :=
291	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨V, hxV⟩ : OpenNhds x))
292	
293	/-- **Germ characterisation of the inner descent** on a section `b` over `V`: the inner
294	descent sends `germ b` to the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
295	private lemma revInner_germ (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
296	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.presheaf.obj (op V))) :
297	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a))
298	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV)) b)
299	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
300	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
301	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
302	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
303	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
304	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) := by
305	  rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_revInner]
306	  exact revInnerLeg_apply A B x U hxU a V hxV b
307	
308	/-- **Outer cocone leg for the reverse map.** For a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the additive map
309	`A(U) → (B.stalk x →+ (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x)` sending an `A`-section `a` to the inner descent
310	`revInner` it determines. Additivity in `a` is checked on germ generators of `B.stalk x`
311	via `revInner_germ` and bilinearity of the section tensor. -/
312	private noncomputable def revOuterLeg (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
313	    (↑(A.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+
314	      ((↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x) : Type u) →+
315	        ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x)) where
316	  toFun a := ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a)
317	  map_add' a a' := by
318	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
319	    intro ξ
320	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
321	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.add_apply, revInner_germ]
322	    erw [_root_.map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul, _root_.map_add]
323	  map_zero' := by
324	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
325	    intro ξ
326	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
327	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.zero_apply, revInner_germ]
328	    erw [_root_.map_zero, TensorProduct.zero_tmul, _root_.map_zero]
329	
330	/-- Evaluation of the outer leg: it is the inner descent. -/
331	private lemma revOuterLeg_apply (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
332	    (ξ : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x)) :
333	    (revOuterLeg A B x U hxU a) ξ = (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a)) ξ :=
334	  rfl
335	
336	/-- **Outer descent of the reverse map.** The additive map
337	`A.stalk x → (B.stalk x →+ (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x)` descending `revOuterLeg` through the
338	`A`-stalk colimit; this is the additive bilinear comparison underlying the reverse map. -/
339	private noncomputable def revBihom :
340	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk A.presheaf x ⟶
341	      AddCommGrpCat.of ((↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x) : Type u) →+
342	        ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x)) :=
343	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ A.presheaf)
344	    ⟨AddCommGrpCat.of _,
345	      fun U => AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (revOuterLeg A B x (unop U).1 (unop U).2),
346	      by
347	        intro W W' f
348	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
349	        ext a
350	        simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
351	        erw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.id_apply]
352	        apply AddMonoidHom.ext
353	        intro ξ
354	        obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
355	        erw [revOuterLeg_apply, revOuterLeg_apply, revInner_germ, revInner_germ]
356	        have hVle : (unop W').1 ≤ (unop W).1 := leOfHom ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).map f.unop)
357	        have j : (unop W').1 ⊓ V ⟶ (unop W).1 ⊓ V := homOfLE (inf_le_inf_right V hVle)
358	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf j x
359	          ⟨(unop W').2, hxV⟩]
360	        congr 1
361	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
362	        congr 1
363	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, ← presheaf_map_apply_coe, Functor.comp_map]
364	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
365	            ← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
366	          congr 1
367	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe]
368	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
369	          congr 1⟩
370	
371	/-- The outer descent factors `revOuterLeg` through the `A`-germ. -/
372	private lemma germ_revBihom (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
373	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU ≫ revBihom A B x
374	      = AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (revOuterLeg A B x U hxU) :=
375	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨U, hxU⟩ : OpenNhds x))
376	
377	/-- **Germ characterisation of the additive bilinear comparison** on germ generators:
378	`revBihom (germ a) (germ b)` is the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
379	private lemma revBihom_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
380	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.obj (op V))) :
381	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x))
382	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU)) a))
383	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV)) b)
384	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
385	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
386	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
387	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
388	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
389	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) := by
390	  have hb : (ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x))
391	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU)) a)
392	      = revOuterLeg A B x U hxU a := by
393	    rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_revBihom]
394	    rfl
395	  rw [hb, revOuterLeg_apply, revInner_germ]
396	
397	/-- **The `W ⊓ W` section identity underlying the stage-(iv) balancing.** Over the
398	self-intersection `W ⊓ W`, restricting a scalar-twisted simple tensor commutes between the
399	two factors. Proved by transporting the clean `W`-level identity `(r₀ • a) ⊗ b = a ⊗ (r₀ • b)`
400	(`TensorProduct.smul_tmul` over the `CommRingCat` carrier `R(W)` — no carrier mixing, the
401	scalar never leaves `R(W)`) down through `(A ⊗ᵖ B).map` via `tensorObj_map_tmul`. This is the
402	project-local resolution of the `CommRingCat`-vs-`RingCat` carrier wall flagged in stage (iii). -/
403	private lemma revBihom_balanced_germ (W Z : Opens X) (hxZ : x ∈ Z) (j : Z ⟶ W)
404	    (r₀ : ↑(R.obj (op W))) (a : ↑(A.obj (op W))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op W))) :
405	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf Z x hxZ))
406	        (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map j.op)) (r₀ • a : ↑(A.obj (op W))) :
407	              ↑(A.obj (op Z)))
408	          ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op Z)]
409	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map j.op)) b : ↑(B.obj (op Z))))
410	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf Z x hxZ))
411	        (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map j.op)) a : ↑(A.obj (op Z)))
412	          ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op Z)]
413	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map j.op)) (r₀ • b : ↑(B.obj (op W))) :
414	              ↑(B.obj (op Z)))) := by
415	  congr 1
416	  have hWsec : ((r₀ • a) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op W)] b : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).obj (op W)))
417	      = a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op W)] (r₀ • b) := TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _
418	  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom ((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).map j.op)) hWsec
419	  erw [tensorObj_map_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul] at h2
420	  exact h2
421	
422	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-balancing of the additive bilinear comparison `revBihom`** — the
423	condition feeding `TensorProduct.liftAddHom` to produce the reverse map. On germ
424	generators over a common neighbourhood `W := T ⊓ U ⊓ V` the germ–scalar compatibility
425	`germ_smul` keeps the scalar in `R.stalk x`, and the two `revBihom_germ_tmul` germs reduce
426	to the single section identity `revBihom_balanced_section` over `W ⊓ W`. -/
427	private lemma revBihom_balanced (r : ↑(R.stalk x))
428	    (m : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk A.presheaf x))
429	    (n : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x)) :
430	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x)) (r • m)) n
431	      = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x)) m) (r • n) := by
432	  obtain ⟨T, hxT, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
433	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, a, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist A.presheaf x m
434	  obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x n
435	  set W : Opens X := T ⊓ U ⊓ V with hWdef
436	  have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨⟨hxT, hxU⟩, hxV⟩
437	  rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R
438	        (homOfLE (le_trans inf_le_left inf_le_left) : W ⟶ T) x hxW r₀,
439	      ← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply A.presheaf
440	        (homOfLE (le_trans inf_le_left inf_le_right) : W ⟶ U) x hxW a,
441	      ← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply B.presheaf
442	        (homOfLE inf_le_right : W ⟶ V) x hxW b]
443	  set r₀' := (ConcreteCategory.hom
444	    (R.map (homOfLE (le_trans inf_le_left inf_le_left) : W ⟶ T).op)) r₀ with hr₀'
445	  set a' := (ConcreteCategory.hom
446	    (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_trans inf_le_left inf_le_right) : W ⟶ U).op)) a with ha'
447	  set b' := (ConcreteCategory.hom
448	    (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : W ⟶ V).op)) b with hb'
449	  rw [← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x W hxW,
450	      ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul B x W hxW,
451	      revBihom_germ_tmul A B x W hxW _ W hxW b',
452	      revBihom_germ_tmul A B x W hxW a' W hxW _]
453	  exact revBihom_balanced_germ A B x W (W ⊓ W) ⟨hxW, hxW⟩ (homOfLE inf_le_left) r₀' a' b'
454	
455	/-- **The reverse map of the d.2 stalk--tensor comparison** `A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x`,
456	the descent of the `R.stalk x`-balanced additive bilinear map `revBihom` through the relative
457	tensor product over `R.stalk x` (via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`). This is the candidate inverse
458	to `stalkTensorLinearMap`; mutual inversion is checked in `stalkTensorIso`. -/
459	noncomputable def stalkTensorRev :
460	    Tgt →+ ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x) :=
461	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom (ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x)) (revBihom_balanced A B x)
462	
463	/-- Germ characterisation of the reverse map on a simple tensor of germs: `germ a ⊗ germ b`
464	maps to the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
465	lemma stalkTensorRev_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
466	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.obj (op V))) :
467	    stalkTensorRev A B x
468	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
469	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV)) b)
470	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
471	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
472	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
473	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
474	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
475	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) := by
476	  rw [stalkTensorRev, TensorProduct.liftAddHom_tmul]
477	  exact revBihom_germ_tmul A B x U hxU a V hxV b
478	
479	/-- **Germ of a restricted simple tensor.** The germ over `Z` of `(a|_Z) ⊗ (b|_Z)` agrees with
480	the germ over `W` of `a ⊗ b`, for `j : Z ⟶ W` and sections `a, b` over `W`. The reverse-map
481	inversion identity `germ_{U⊓U}((a|) ⊗ (b|)) = germ_U (a ⊗ b)` is the special case `Z = U ⊓ U`.
482	Stated with a single restriction `j` (so the `tensorObj_map_tmul` fold applies); the germ wrapper
483	supplies the expected module type for the relative tensor over the `CommRingCat` carrier `R(Z)`. -/
484	private lemma germ_tensorObj_map_tmul (W Z : Opens X) (hxZ : x ∈ Z) (hxW : x ∈ W) (j : Z ⟶ W)
485	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op W))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op W))) :
486	    (ConcreteCategory.hom
487	        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf Z x hxZ))
488	        (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map j.op)) a : ↑(A.obj (op Z))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op Z)]
489	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map j.op)) b : ↑(B.obj (op Z))))
490	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
491	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf W x hxW))
492	          ((a : ↑(A.obj (op W))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op W)] (b : ↑(B.obj (op W)))) := by
493	  erw [← PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul (M₁ := A) (M₂ := B) j.op a b,
494	    ← presheaf_map_apply_coe (Monoidal.tensorObj A B)]
495	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf j x hxZ _
496	
497	/-- **The d.2 stalk--tensor commutation isomorphism** (blueprint `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`).
498	The `R.stalk x`-linear comparison map `stalkTensorLinearMap` is an isomorphism
499	`(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x ≃ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, with inverse the descended reverse map `stalkTensorRev`.
500	Mutual inversion is checked on germ generators (`stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul`,
501	`stalkTensorRev_germ_tmul`) and extended by `TensorProduct.induction_on` and the joint
502	epimorphism of the germ maps (`TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext`). This is the single
503	genuinely Mathlib-absent ingredient (d.2) underlying the unconditional associator of the
504	relative-Picard tensor substrate. -/
505	noncomputable def stalkTensorIso :
506	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x) : Type u)
507	      ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)] Tgt where
508	  toFun := stalkTensorLinearMap A B x
509	  map_add' := map_add _
510	  map_smul' := (stalkTensorLinearMap A B x).map_smul'
511	  invFun := stalkTensorRev A B x
512	  left_inv := by
513	    intro s
514	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, w, rfl⟩ :=
515	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x s
516	    induction w using TensorProduct.induction_on with
517	    | zero =>
518	        erw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
519	    | tmul a b =>
520	        erw [stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul, stalkTensorRev_germ_tmul]
521	        exact germ_tensorObj_map_tmul A B x U (U ⊓ U) ⟨hxU, hxU⟩ hxU (homOfLE inf_le_left) a b
522	    | add p q hp hq =>
523	        erw [map_add, map_add, map_add, hp, hq]
524	  right_inv := by
525	    intro t
526	    induction t using TensorProduct.induction_on with
527	    | zero =>
528	        simp only [map_zero]
529	    | tmul ξ η =>
530	        obtain ⟨U, hxU, a, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist A.presheaf x ξ
531	        obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x η
532	        rw [stalkTensorRev_germ_tmul]
533	        erw [stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul,
534	            TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply A.presheaf
535	              (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩,
536	            TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply B.presheaf
537	              (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩]
538	    | add p q hp hq =>
539	        simp only [map_add]
540	        rw [hp, hq]
541	
542	end StalkTensor
543	
544	end PresheafOfModules

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.StalkTensor
8	
9	/-!
10	# Vestigial / quarantine sub-module of `TensorObjSubstrate`
11	
12	This file quarantines three sections of the substrate build that are NOT on the
13	primary `exists_tensorObj_inverse` closure path but may serve future prover work:
14	
15	- `FlatWhisker` / `WhiskerOfW` (route-(e) flatness-free whiskering; one open sorry
16	  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`)
17	- `StalkLinearMap` (route-(e) d.1 ingredient; axiom-clean)
18	- `OverSliceSheafEquiv` (the shared root for both ⊗-inverse bridges; axiom-clean)
19	
20	Imported by `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate` (the public API file).
21	-/
22	
23	set_option autoImplicit false
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory
28	
29	namespace PresheafOfModules
30	
31	universe v'
32	
33	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
34	
35	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
36	sheafification localizer
37	
38	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
39	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
40	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
41	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
42	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
43	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
44	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
45	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
46	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
47	structure. -/
48	
49	section FlatWhisker
50	
51	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
52	
53	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
54	
55	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
56	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
57	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
58	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
59	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
60	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
61	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
62	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
63	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
64	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
65	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
66	
67	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
68	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
69	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
70	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
71	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
72	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
73	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
74	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
75	
76	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
77	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
78	half. -/
79	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
80	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
81	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
82	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
83	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
84	  constructor
85	  intro U s
86	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
87	  | zero =>
88	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
89	      intro V i _
90	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
91	  | tmul a b =>
92	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
93	      intro V i hi
94	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
95	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
96	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
97	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
98	      congr 1
99	  | add s t hs ht =>
100	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
101	      intro V i hi
102	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
103	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
104	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
105	
106	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
107	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
108	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
109	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
110	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
111	injectivity half. -/
112	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
113	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
114	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
115	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
116	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
117	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
118	  constructor
119	  intro X ξ η h
120	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
121	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
122	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
123	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
124	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
125	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
126	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
127	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
128	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
129	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
130	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
131	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
132	  have hex : Function.Exact
133	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
134	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
135	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
136	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
137	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
138	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
139	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
140	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
141	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
142	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
143	    intro ζ
144	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
145	    | zero =>
146	        rw [map_zero]
147	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
148	    | tmul a kk =>
149	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
150	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
151	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
152	          rw [map_zero]
153	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
154	          exact kk.2
155	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
156	        intro V f hf
157	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
158	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
159	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
160	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
161	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
162	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
163	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
164	        rw [map_add]
165	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
166	        intro V f hf
167	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
168	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
169	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
170	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
171	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
172	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
173	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
174	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
175	  intro V f hf
176	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
177	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
178	  exact hf
179	
180	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
181	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
182	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
183	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
184	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
185	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
186	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
187	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
188	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
189	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
190	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
191	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
192	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
193	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
194	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
195	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
196	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
197	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
198	
199	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
200	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
201	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
202	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
203	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
204	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
205	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
206	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
207	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
208	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
209	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
210	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
211	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
212	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
213	  have hconj : g ▷ F
214	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
215	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
216	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
217	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
218	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
219	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
220	  exact hwl
221	
222	
223	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
224	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
225	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
226	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
227	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
228	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
229	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
230	read at a single morphism. -/
231	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
232	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
233	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
234	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
235	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
236	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
237	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
238	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
239	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
240	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
241	  exact h2
242	
243	end FlatWhisker
244	
245	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
246	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
247	
248	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
249	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
250	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
251	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
252	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
253	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
254	
255	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
256	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
257	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
258	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
259	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
260	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
261	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
262	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
263	
264	section StalkLinearMap
265	
266	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
267	
268	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
269	
270	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
271	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
272	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
273	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
274	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
275	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
276	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
277	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
278	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
279	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
280	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
281	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
282	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
283	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
284	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
285	  map_smul' r s := by
286	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
287	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
288	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
289	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
290	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
291	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
292	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
293	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
294	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
295	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
296	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
297	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
298	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
299	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
300	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
301	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
302	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
303	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
304	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
305	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
306	      intro z
307	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
308	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
309	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
310	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
311	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
312	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
313	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
314	
315	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
316	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
317	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
318	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
319	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
320	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
321	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
322	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
323	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
324	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
325	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
326	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
327	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
328	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
329	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
330	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
331	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
332	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
333	
334	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
335	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
336	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
337	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
338	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
339	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
340	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
341	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
342	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
343	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
344	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
345	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
346	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
347	
348	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
349	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
350	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
351	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
352	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
353	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
354	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
355	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
356	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
357	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
358	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
359	
360	end StalkLinearMap
361	
362	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — stalk bridges for the topological site
363	
364	For the topological site `Opens X`, a morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete
365	category is locally injective iff it is injective on all stalks. Mathlib supplies the
366	*surjective* analogue for presheaves (`locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`) and
367	the *injective* one only at the sheaf level (`app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`);
368	the presheaf-level local-injectivity bridge is project-local. Combined, they characterise
369	the sheafification localizer `J.W` stalkwise (`isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W`), the (d.1)
370	ingredient of the route-(d) whiskering closure. -/
371	
372	section StalkBridge
373	
374	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
375	
376	universe w
377	
378	variable {A : Type w} [Category.{u} A] [Limits.HasColimits A]
379	  {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type u} [(P Q : A) → FunLike (FA P Q) (CA P) (CA Q)]
380	  [ConcreteCategory A FA] [Limits.PreservesFilteredColimits (forget A)]
381	  {Z : TopCat.{u}}
382	
383	/-- **Injective on stalks ⇒ locally injective (topological site).** For a morphism `T` of
384	presheaves valued in a concrete category over a topological space `Z`, stalkwise injectivity
385	implies local injectivity for `Opens.grothendieckTopology Z`. Project-local: Mathlib provides
386	this only at the sheaf level (`app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`); here it is the
387	presheaf-level statement, proved directly through `germ_eq` (the equalizer sieve covers each
388	point because agreeing germs agree on a neighbourhood). -/
389	theorem isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk
390	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf A Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
391	    (h : ∀ z : Z, Function.Injective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((stalkFunctor A z).map T))) :
392	    CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) T := by
393	  refine ⟨fun {U'} s t hst z hz => ?_⟩
394	  have hgerm : (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ (unop U') z hz)) s
395	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ (unop U') z hz)) t := by
396	    apply h z
397	    rw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, hst]
398	  obtain ⟨W, hzW, iU, iV, heq⟩ := F.germ_eq z hz hz s t hgerm
399	  refine ⟨W, iU, ?_, hzW⟩
400	  rw [Subsingleton.elim iV iU] at heq
401	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply]
402	  exact heq
403	
404	/-- **Locally injective ⇒ injective on stalks (topological site).** The converse of
405	`isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk`: a locally injective morphism of presheaves over a
406	topological space induces injective stalk maps. Proved via `germ_eq` and the covering of the
407	equalizer sieve. -/
408	theorem injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective
409	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf A Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
410	    (hT : CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) T)
411	    (z : Z) :
412	    Function.Injective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((stalkFunctor A z).map T)) := by
413	  intro sₓ tₓ hxeq
414	  obtain ⟨U, hzU, s, rfl⟩ := F.germ_exist z sₓ
415	  obtain ⟨V, hzV, t, rfl⟩ := F.germ_exist z tₓ
416	  rw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply] at hxeq
417	  obtain ⟨W, hzW, iU, iV, heqG⟩ := G.germ_eq z hzU hzV
418	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op U))) s) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op V))) t) hxeq
419	  set a := (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.map iU.op)) s with ha
420	  set b := (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.map iV.op)) t with hb
421	  have hTab : (ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op W))) a
422	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op W))) b := by
423	    rw [ha, hb, NatTrans.naturality_apply T iU.op s, NatTrans.naturality_apply T iV.op t]
424	    exact heqG
425	  obtain ⟨W', f, hf, hzW'⟩ := hT.equalizerSieve_mem (X := op W) a b hTab z hzW
426	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf
427	  have e3 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ W z hzW)) a
428	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ W z hzW)) b := by
429	    rw [← F.germ_res_apply f z hzW' a, ← F.germ_res_apply f z hzW' b, hf]
430	  rw [← F.germ_res_apply iU z hzW s, ← F.germ_res_apply iV z hzW t, ← ha, ← hb, e3]
431	
432	/-- **The sheafification localizer is a stalkwise isomorphism (topological site).** For
433	presheaves of abelian groups on a topological space `Z` whose Grothendieck topology has
434	`WEqualsLocallyBijective`, a morphism in the sheafification localizer `J.W` induces an
435	isomorphism on every stalk. This is the (d.1) bridge: it combines the injective stalk bridge
436	`injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective` with Mathlib's surjective one
437	`locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`, and `ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective`. -/
438	theorem isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W {Z : TopCat.{u}}
439	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology Z).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
440	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf AddCommGrpCat.{u} Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
441	    (hT : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z).W T) (z : Z) :
442	    IsIso ((stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map T) := by
443	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hT
444	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
445	  exact ⟨injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective T hT.1 z,
446	    (TopCat.Presheaf.locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks T).mp hT.2 z⟩
447	
448	end StalkBridge
449	
450	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
451	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), realized via the stalk–tensor commutation d.2)
452	
453	The flat whiskering `W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat` needs the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
454	`∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible sheaves over non-affine opens
455	and is therefore OFF the associator critical path. The associator only ever whiskers the
456	sheafification UNIT `η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`). Whiskering a
457	*locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves local bijectivity with NO flatness
458	hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism
459	(d.1, `isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W`), the tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism by the
460	stalk–tensor commutation (d.2, `stalkTensorIso`) — flatness-free, because *isomorphisms*
461	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. -/
462	
463	section WhiskerOfW
464	
465	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
466	open PresheafOfModules.Monoidal
467	
468	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
469	
470	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity (flatness-free).**
471	For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g` whose underlying additive map is
472	locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective. The
473	proof is the stalkwise computation `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: by (d.1) `g_x` is an
474	isomorphism, and by the (d.2) stalk–tensor commutation `stalkTensorIso` the stalk of `F ◁ g`
475	is identified with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`, hence an isomorphism — for *any* `F`, with neither
476	sectionwise flatness nor local triviality of `F`. This is the single residual ingredient of the
477	associator `tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). -/
478	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W
479	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
480	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
481	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
482	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
483	    IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (F ◁ g) := by
484	  -- (d.1) `g` is a stalkwise isomorphism.
485	  have hgx : ∀ z : X, IsIso ((stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :=
486	    fun z => isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W _ hg z
487	  -- Reduce to stalkwise injectivity of `F ◁ g`.
488	  apply isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk
489	  intro z
490	  -- The (d.2) stalk–tensor commutation isomorphisms and the stalkwise iso of `g`.
491	  set eM := stalkTensorIso F M z with heM
492	  set eN := stalkTensorIso F N z with heN
493	  set e := stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso g z (hgx z) with he
494	  set E := TensorProduct.congr
495	    (LinearEquiv.refl (↑(R.stalk z)) (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk F.presheaf z))) e with hE
496	  set φ := (stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) with hφ
497	  -- B-naturality: under `eM`/`eN`, the stalk of `F ◁ g` is `id_{F_z} ⊗ g_z`.
498	  have key : ∀ w : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf z),
499	      eN (ConcreteCategory.hom φ w) = E (eM w) := by
500	    intro w
501	    obtain ⟨W, hzW, p, rfl⟩ :=
502	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf z w
503	    -- The germ map, `φ`, `eN`, `eM`, `E` are all additive; the `+`/`0` instances on the
504	    -- defeq carriers `(toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)` vs `(Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf` differ
505	    -- syntactically, so we distribute `map_add`/`map_zero` via `have`-terms (defeq-coerced).
506	    induction p using TensorProduct.induction_on with
507	    | zero =>
508	        erw [map_zero (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)),
509	          map_zero (ConcreteCategory.hom φ), map_zero eN, map_zero eM, map_zero E]
510	    | tmul a m =>
511	        -- RHS: `eM (germ (a ⊗ m)) = germ_F a ⊗ germ_M m`, then `E` sends it to
512	        -- `germ_F a ⊗ e (germ_M m) = germ_F a ⊗ germ_N (g_W m)`.
513	        have hRM : eM ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW))
514	              (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))] m))
515	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ F.presheaf W z hzW)) a
516	              ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk z)]
517	                (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W z hzW)) m :=
518	          stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul F M z W hzW a m
519	        have hLN : eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F N).presheaf W z hzW))
520	              (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))]
521	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)))
522	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ F.presheaf W z hzW)) a
523	              ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk z)]
524	                (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W z hzW))
525	                  ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m) :=
526	          stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul F N z W hzW a ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)
527	        have hL1 : (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
528	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW))
529	                (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))] m))
530	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F N).presheaf W z hzW))
531	                (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))]
532	                  ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)) := by
533	          erw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]
534	          congr 1
535	        rw [hL1, hLN, hRM, hE, TensorProduct.congr_tmul, LinearEquiv.refl_apply, he]
536	        congr 1
537	        exact (stalkLinearMap_germ g z W hzW m).symm
538	    | add p q hp hq =>
539	        have hg2 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) (p + q)
540	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p
541	              + (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q :=
542	          map_add _ _ _
543	        rw [hg2]
544	        have hphi2 : (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
545	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p
546	                + (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q)
547	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
548	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p)
549	              + (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
550	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q) :=
551	          map_add _ _ _
552	        rw [hphi2]
553	        have heN2 : eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
554	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p)
555	            + (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
556	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q))
557	            = eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
558	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p))
559	              + eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
560	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q)) :=
561	          map_add _ _ _
562	        rw [heN2]
563	        simp only [map_add]
564	        rw [hp, hq]
565	  -- Hence the stalk map of `F ◁ g` is a composite of bijections.
566	  have hbij : Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom φ) := by
567	    have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom φ) = ⇑eN.symm ∘ ⇑E ∘ ⇑eM := by
568	      funext w
569	      rw [Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, ← key w, eN.symm_apply_apply]
570	    rw [hfun]
571	    exact eN.symm.bijective.comp (E.bijective.comp eM.bijective)
572	  exact hbij.1
573	
574	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The ROUTE (d)
575	replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a locally bijective `g`
576	(`∈ J.W`), `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`. Local surjectivity is free
577	(`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness); local injectivity is the flatness-free
578	stalkwise residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
579	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W
580	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
581	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
582	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
583	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
584	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
585	  have hbij := hg
586	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
587	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
588	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
589	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
590	
591	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
592	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
593	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W
594	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
595	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
596	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
597	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
598	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
599	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
600	  have hconj : g ▷ F
601	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
602	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
603	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
604	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
605	  rw [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W.cancel_left_of_respectsIso,
606	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W.cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
607	  exact hwl
608	
609	end WhiskerOfW
610	
611	end PresheafOfModules
612	
613	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site
614	equivalence (the SHARED root of both ⊗-inverse bridges)
615	
616	For a topological space `X` and an open `U : Opens X`, Mathlib supplies the
617	1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
618	(`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`) but leaves, as a documented `## TODO`, the
619	upgrade of this to an *equivalence of sheaf categories*
620	
621	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
622	
623	This is the single Mathlib-absent root on which BOTH remaining ⊗-inverse bridges
624	(the A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` gluing and the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
625	reduce: re-evaluating a slice-internal-hom along the open immersion `U ↪ X` is
626	exactly transport across this sheaf-site equivalence. It is value-category
627	parametric (`A` is arbitrary), so one build serves both lanes.
628	
629	The proof routes through `CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, which needs only
630	the dense-subsite instance `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite …`. The sole
631	non-formal content of that instance is the cover-correspondence
632	`functorPushforward_mem_iff`, which we discharge *pointwise*: on the thin poset
633	`Opens X` a covering sieve is a pointwise neighbourhood cover, and the open
634	embedding `↥U ↪ X` matches points and opens on both sides. No `Over.map`
635	pseudofunctor coherence appears — thinness trivialises it.
636	
637	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
638	
639	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
640	
641	section OverSliceSheafEquiv
642	
643	open Topology CategoryTheory
644	
645	universe v w
646	
647	-- NOTE: within the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` namespace the unqualified
648	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
649	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
650	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
651	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
652	
653	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
654	
655	/-- Pointwise cover-correspondence underlying `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`: a
656	sieve `S` on `W : Opens ↥U` is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its
657	pushforward along the open-embedding image functor `↥U ↪ X` is a covering sieve in
658	`X`. Both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition of
659	`Opens.grothendieckTopology`, matched across the injection `Subtype.val`. -/
660	private lemma overEquiv_image_cover_iff (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥U) (S : Sieve W) :
661	    (S.functorPushforward ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U))
662	        ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
663	          (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W)
664	      ↔ S ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) W := by
665	  constructor
666	  · intro h y hy
667	    have hx : (y : X) ∈
668	        (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W) :=
669	      ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
670	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hxV⟩ := h y hx
671	    obtain ⟨W', a, b, hSa, _⟩ := hf
672	    refine ⟨W', a, hSa, ?_⟩
673	    have hyim : (y : X) ∈
674	        ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W' := b.le hxV
675	    obtain ⟨z, hz, hzeq⟩ := hyim
676	    rw [← (Subtype.ext hzeq : z = y)]; exact hz
677	  · intro h x hx
678	    obtain ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩ := hx
679	    obtain ⟨W', a, hSa, hyW'⟩ := h y hy
680	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
681	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
682	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
683	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
684	
685	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a
686	dense subsite of `(Over U, slice topology)`. This is the dense-subsite datum
687	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
688	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
689	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
690	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
691	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
692	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
693	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
694	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
695	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
696	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
697	        = S.functorPushforward
698	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
699	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
700	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S
701	
702	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
703	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
704	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
705	
706	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
707	
708	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
709	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
710	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
711	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
712	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
713	
714	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
715	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
716	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
717	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
718	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
719	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
720	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
721	
722	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
723	
724	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
725

  "statement": "ok\n[Sheafification inverts a localizer morphism of presheaves of modules]\n  \n  \n  Let \\(R_0\\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a\n  Grothendieck topology \\(J\\), let \\(R_{sh}\\) be its sheafification, and let\n  \\(\\alpha : R_0 \\to R_{sh}.\\mathrm{obj}\\) be the (locally bijective) comparison\n  map of presheaves of rings. Let \\(f : A \\to B\\) be a morphism of presheaves of\n  \\(R_0\\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups\n  \\((\\mathtt{toPresheaf}\\,R_0).\\mathrm{map}\\,f\\) lies in the sheafification\n  localizer class \\(J.W\\) --- equivalently, is locally bijective for \\(J\\). Then\n  the presheaf-of-modules sheafification functor\n  \\(\\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\\,\\alpha\\) sends \\(f\\) to an\n  isomorphism:\n  \\[\n    (\\mathtt{sheafification}\\,\\alpha).\\mathrm{map}\\,f\n    \\quad\\text{is an isomorphism.}\n  \\]\n  This is a closed go/no-go bridge --- it converts ``\\,a morphism lies in\n  \\(J.W\\)\\,'' into ``\\,its sheafification is invertible\\,''. Under route~(e) the\n  associator is the API-derived one (\\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), so this bridge is\n  retained as a supplement rather than as the associator's load-bearing step.",
  "uses": [],
  "lean_name": "PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W",
  "proved": true,
  "mathlib_ok": false,
  "proof_tex": "\n\n\\leanok\n  This is a one-morphism reading of the structural identity that the\n  sheafification of presheaves of \\(R_0\\)-modules \\emph{is} the localization of\n  \\(\\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\\,R_0\\) at the class\n  \\(J.W.\\mathtt{inverseImage}\\,(\\mathtt{toPresheaf}\\,R_0)\\) of morphisms whose\n  underlying map of presheaves of abelian groups is locally bijective. Concretely,\n  the underlying \\(\\mathtt{AddCommGrp}\\)-valued sheafification inverts exactly the\n  locally bijective morphisms, and Mathlib's\n  \\(\\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.inverseImage\\_W\\_toPresheaf\\_eq\\_inverseImage\\_\n  isomorphisms}\\) identifies the preimage of \\(J.W\\) under\n  \\(\\mathtt{toPresheaf}\\,R_0\\) with the preimage of the class of isomorphisms under\n  the module-level sheafification functor. Reading that equality of morphism\n  classes at the single morphism \\(f\\): the hypothesis\n  \\(J.W\\,\\bigl((\\mathtt{toPresheaf}\\,R_0).\\mathrm{map}\\,f\\bigr)\\) places \\(f\\) in\n  that preimage, hence \\((\\mathtt{sheafification}\\,\\alpha).\\mathrm{map}\\,f\\) is an\n  isomorphism. No new construction is required beyond instantiating the existing\n  Mathlib identity at one morphism; this lemma is a closed thin wrapper, retained\n  as a supplement under route~(e).\n",
  "lean_source": "lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W\n    {R\u2080 : C\u1d52\u1d56 \u2964 RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (\u03b1 : R\u2080 \u27f6 Rsh.obj)\n    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J \u03b1] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J \u03b1]\n    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]\n    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R\u2080} (f : A \u27f6 B)\n    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R\u2080).map f)) :\n    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification \u03b1).map f) := by\n  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) \u03b1\n  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage\n      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification \u03b1) f := by rw [\u2190 h]; exact hf\n  exact h2",
